title: Injuring or defiling place of worship, with intent to insult the religion of any class
content: Whoever destroys, damages or defiles any place of worship, or any object held sacred by any class of persons with the intention of thereby insulting the religion of any class of persons or with the knowledge that any class of persons is likely to consider such destruction, damage or defilement as an insult to their religion, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.

sentencing_ladder.penalty: Up to 2 years imprisonment, or fine, or both

footnotes.text: Mens Rea Requirement: This offense requires a specific intent to insult a religion, or subjective knowledge that a class of persons will view the physical act as an insult to their faith.

title: Use of derogatory remarks, etc., in respect of the Holy Prophet
content: Whoever by words, either spoken or written, or by visible representation, or by any imputation, innuendo, or insinuation, directly or indirectly, defiles the sacred name of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) shall be punished with death, and shall also be liable to fine.

footnotes.text: Statutory History & Interpretation: Inserted via the Criminal Law (Amendment) Act, 1986. The Federal Shariat Court later ruled in 'Ismail Qureshi v. Union of Pakistan' (PLD 1991 FSC 10) that death is the alternative-free, mandatory penalty for conviction under this section.
